---
name: Catalog vocabulary or curation
about: Propose changes to manifest vocabulary, label families, source coverage, duplicate handling, or recurring coverage skew.
title: "[Catalog curation]: "
assignees: ""
---

## Issue Type

Catalog vocabulary, coverage skew, duplicate review, source trail, relabeling, or cross-linking.

Use this template when repeated local friction points to a catalog-level pattern that could improve retrieval, authoring, or maintenance for the community.

## Summary

Describe the curation problem in one short paragraph. Name the recurring retrieval, authoring, labeling, or source-tracing need.

## Evidence From Use

- Task or chart context:
- Retrieval path:
- Pattern observed across records or searches:
- What was missing, hard to retrieve, ambiguous, duplicate, or misleading:
- Why this appears to be a catalog curation issue rather than a tooling issue:

## Related Guideline Records

List verified guideline ids when the issue touches existing records. Use `None found` for vocabulary or coverage gaps without matching records.

- `<guideline-id>`: `<title>`
  - Exact-read command:
  - Relevant section:
  - Why it partially applies or shows the curation problem:
  - Citation output:

## Suggested Catalog Change

Use the lines that fit.

- Clarify manifest vocabulary:
- Add or revise label families:
- Add or revise labels:
- Add cross-links:
- Review duplicate or overlapping records:
- Add or check source coverage:

## Public Disclosure Check

- [ ] No private user data.
- [ ] No local filesystem paths.
- [ ] No unpublished screenshots or session artifacts unless intentionally attached.
- [ ] Any referenced guideline ids were verified with exact reads.
- [ ] Source-backed records were checked with `chartcoach catalog cite` when references are discussed.

## Uncertainty

What should a catalog maintainer inspect before changing catalog structure or vocabulary?
